Benefits.benefit1Description: Targeted fatty acid profiles selected to maintain barrier integrity, dermal health, and coat quality.
Benefits.benefit5Description: Fiber composition selected to deliver firm, predictable stools—an observable marker of digestive function.
Benefits.benefit4Description: Fermentable fibers and polyphenols selected to encourage microbial balance and short-chain fatty acid production.
Benefits.benefit2Description: Macronutrient ratios selected to enable slow, steady glucose release and prevent energy fluctuation.
Benefits.benefit3Description: Soluble fibers and digestible proteins selected to promote nutrient absorption and gastrointestinal tolerance.
Benefits.description: Human-grade, pumpkin-based meals for your furry friends. Includes one puree and pre-portioned, tailored ingredients to easily cook up fresh, nutritious meals.

ClinicallyApproved.disclaimerText: IACUC (Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee) is the oversight body responsible for reviewing and approving research protocols involving animals. AAALAC (Association for Assessment and Accreditation of Laboratory Animal Care) is an independent organization that accredits institutions meeting the highest standards of animal care and welfare in research settings.
ClinicallyApproved.title: Clinically evaluated in Cornell University-led research
ClinicallyApproved.description: Our formulation underwent an eight-week preclinical feeding evaluation at Cornell University to understand how our formulation interacts with digestion, metabolism, and physiological markers in adult dogs.
ClinicallyApproved.item1Description: Daily fecal scoring conducted using the Purina 1–7 scale. Consistency and gastrointestinal tolerance tracked across the full eight-week period.
ClinicallyApproved.item2Description: Food acceptance measured twice daily. Monitored both immediate intake and total consumption to assess real-world feeding behavior.
ClinicallyApproved.item3Description: Weekly assessments of body weight, body condition (AAHA 9-point scale), and muscle condition (WSAVA) completed. All evaluations conducted by veterinary staff blinded to diet group.
ClinicallyApproved.item4Description: Bloodwork performed at study start and conclusion. Complete blood count, chemistry panel, CRP, fructosamine, and serum IL-6 were measured.
ClinicallyApproved.item5Description: Skin, coat, oral health, and aural cytology evaluated at multiple timepoints. Standardized veterinary scoring systems used for objectivity.
ClinicallyApproved.item6Description: Conducted under an approved IACUC protocol at Cornell's AAALAC-accredited Preclinical Services Core. Full compliance with animal welfare and research standards.

FAQ.decorativeLeftPepperAlt: Decorative pepper illustration on the left
FAQ.decorativeLeftVeggieAlt: Decorative vegetable illustration on the left
FAQ.decorativeRightPepperAlt: Decorative pepper illustration on the right
FAQ.decorativeRightVeggieAlt: Decorative vegetable illustration on the right
FAQ.faq1Question: How is PGF different from other fresh dog foods?
FAQ.faq1Answer: PGF is a clinical nutrition platform, not a lifestyle brand. Formulations are developed with board-certified veterinary nutritionists, validated through controlled research at Cornell, and verified at the batch level. We measure physiological outcomes, not just ingredient quality.
FAQ.faq2Answer: Yes. Every recipe meets or exceeds AAFCO adult maintenance standards, meaning it’s suitable for long-term feeding. But we go further: each production batch is tested against vitamin and mineral targets to confirm nutritional adequacy in the finished food.
FAQ.faq3Question: Which dogs is PGF formulated for?
FAQ.faq3Answer: PGF is currently formulated specifically for small-breed dogs under 20 lbs. Small breeds have faster digestive transit, higher stool variability, and greater nutrient sensitivity. Our recipes are calibrated for this physiology, not scaled down from larger-breed formulations.
FAQ.faq4Question: Is a veterinary prescription required for PGF?
FAQ.faq4Answer: No. PGF is formulated for adult maintenance, not therapeutic use. We recommend discussing any dietary change with your veterinarian, and our nutrition team is available for consultation.
FAQ.faq5Question: What changes do owners typically observe first?
FAQ.faq5Answer: Improved stool consistency, energy stability, coat condition, and digestive tolerance.
FAQ.faq6Question: How flexible is the feeding schedule?
FAQ.faq6Answer: Fully. Adjust delivery cadence, pause, or modify your protocol at any time. No lock-ins.
FAQ.title: Frequently Asked Questions

Features.feature1Title: Verified nutrient content in every meal
Features.feature1Description: We validate every recipe at the finished-food level, using laboratory testing to confirm vitamin and mineral targets are repeatedly met—not just assumed.
Features.feature2Description: Every batch we produce is tested against AAFCO vitamin and mineral requirements, ensuring a consistent nutritional profile despite natural variation in sourcing, seasonality, and raw material composition.
Features.feature3Description: Our formulation development and review are led by a board-certified veterinary nutritionist (ECVCN), ensuring that each decision is guided by established clinical nutrition principles.
Features.feature4Description: Small breeds don’t just eat less, they metabolize differently—our recipes are engineered for their faster digestive transit, higher stool variability, and greater nutrient sensitivity.
Features.feature5Description: We responsibly source our ingredients and select our packaging to balance food safety, nutritional protection, and environmental impact without compromising formulation integrity.

GoldenMeals.description: A protein rationale that accounts for different tolerances, sensitivities, and requirements.
GoldenMeals.imageAlt: Golden meals background with fresh ingredients
GoldenMeals.title: Nutrition that recognizes physiological nuance

Hero.headline: Clinical-grade nutrition for small-breed digestive health
Hero.subheadline: Balanced meals formulated for your dog’s physiology. Limited enrollment now open for our founding cohort.

HowItWorks.step1Description: We start with your dog's physiology, not preferences. Small-breed dogs metabolize differently, meaning our formulation decisions must begin there. We consider size, age, activity level, and digestive sensitivity.
HowItWorks.step2Description: We translate your dog’s needs into a feeding plan grounded in clinical nutrition standards. We map nutrient ratios and portions to support balanced nutrition, digestive stability, and sustained energy.
HowItWorks.step3Title: Your dog receives precision nutrition in every meal
HowItWorks.step3Description: Every meal is prepared under strict protocols. We use measured inputs, controlled cooking, and batch-level nutrient verification to ensure that what we formulated is exactly what gets delivered each time.
HowItWorks.title: How it Works

Detail.lambParagraph1: Our Lamb Recipe features premium New Zealand lamb as the primary protein source, paired with easily digestible carbohydrates and essential nutrients for dogs with sensitivities or those who prefer a novel protein.
Detail.lambParagraph2: Novel protein source for dogs with food sensitivities. Easily digestible carbohydrates for gentle digestion. Essential nutrients for overall health and vitality.
Detail.lambParagraph3: Premium New Zealand lamb, easily digestible carbohydrates, essential nutrients, and carefully selected supplements.
Detail.seafoodParagraph1: Our Seafood Recipe combines wild-caught salmon and whitefish to deliver exceptional omega-3 fatty acids, supporting cognitive function, joint health, and a lustrous coat.
Detail.seafoodParagraph2: Omega-3 fatty acids for cognitive function and joint health. Wild-caught fish for a lustrous coat and skin health. Balanced nutrition for active and senior dogs.
Detail.seafoodParagraph3: Wild-caught salmon, whitefish, omega-3 rich ingredients, and carefully selected supplements.
Detail.turkeyParagraph1: A lean protein formulation calibrated for adult small-breed physiology, delivering controlled fat inclusion to support digestive tolerance and energy balance. Turkey provides a complete amino acid profile optimized for nutrient utilization, with a measured fat-to-protein ratio suited for adult maintenance and weight stability.
Detail.turkeyParagraph2: Optimized amino acid density for lean muscle maintenance and nutrient utilization. Calibrated fiber composition for small-breed digestive stability and gut integrity. Functional bioactives to support microbiome diversity and reduce inflammation.
Detail.turkeyParagraph3: Ground turkey (93% lean), butternut squash, pumpkin, spinach, hard-boiled egg, beef liver, chicken liver, blueberries, flaxseeds, chia seeds, omega-3 fish oil, ground turmeric, ground ginger, PGF vitamin and mineral premix.

EnrollmentCTA.sentence: Now enrolling dogs into our founding cohort in Boston

GuaranteedAnalysis.seafoodIntro: Complete nutrient breakdown for our Seafood Recipe formulation.

GuaranteedAnalysis.turkeyIntro: We provide full nutrient transparency beyond standard labeling requirements. Values are shown both as-fed and on a dry matter basis to support veterinary review and clinical evaluation.

NutritionPanel.aminoAcidsSubhead: Complete essential and non-essential amino acid spectrum to support lean tissue maintenance and metabolic function.
NutritionPanel.fatsSubhead: Fatty acid distribution designed to support skin barrier integrity, inflammatory balance, and microbiome interaction.
NutritionPanel.mineralsSubhead: Essential macro and trace minerals calibrated to support skeletal integrity, electrolyte balance, and cellular function.
NutritionPanel.title: Detailed Nutrition Panel
NutritionPanel.vitaminsSubhead: Fat- and water-soluble vitamins included at calibrated levels to support antioxidant defense and metabolic stability.

turkey.FormulationLogicDescription1: A complete amino acids spectrum optimized for digestibility and nutrient utilization. The fat-to-protein ratio supports lean muscle maintenance while maintaining digestive tolerance in small-breed adults.
turkey.FormulationLogicDescription2: Moderate fat inclusion supports energy stability and weight balance in adult small-breed physiology. Omega-3 fish oil contributes essential EPA and DHA to support inflammatory balance and skin barrier integrity.
turkey.FormulationLogicDescription3: Pumpkin and selected fermentable fibers are included to promote predictable stool consistency and support short-chain fatty acid production, including butyrate, within the gut-immune axis.
turkey.FormulationLogicDescription4: A custom-engineered vitamin and mineral premix calibrated for absorption efficiency and trace mineral bioavailability. Included chelated minerals to support optimal nutrient utilization and systemic balance.
turkey.FormulationLogicDescription5: Polyphenol-rich ingredients contribute antioxidant activity and support microbial diversity and gut integrity within the broader formulation architecture.

bodyShape.heading: How would you describe your dog's body condition?

lightLean.title: Underweight
lightLean.description: Ribs and spine visible or easily felt; minimal fat cover; clear waist and abdominal tuck.

idealBalanced.title: Ideal
idealBalanced.description: Ribs palpable under light fat cover; visible waist from above; healthy abdominal tuck.

slightlyFilled.title: Overweight
slightlyFilled.description: Ribs difficult to feel; waist less defined; abdominal tuck reduced.

fullRound.title: Obese
fullRound.description: No palpable ribs; no visible waist; abdomen distended or pendulous.

BlogIndex.heroSubheadline: Evidence-led perspectives on canine nutrition, physiology, and the standards behind what most labels don’t explain.

BlogPostPage.disclaimer: Educational only. Not medical advice. Consult your veterinarian for individual guidance.

PromiseOfCareAlert.message: Our Commitment: Every formulation meets clinical-grade standards for quality, digestibility, and nutritional adequacy. If physiological response differs from expectations, our Nutrition Concierge will connect with you to gather the additional information necessary to update the feeding profile accordingly.

Hero.title: Our Formulation
Hero.subtitle: Nutrition guided by clinical reasoning
Hero.backgroundAlt: Raw natural ingredients: poultry, egg, pumpkin, meat, ginger, and leafy greens arranged on a light background

Introduction.column1: Developed with board-certified veterinary nutritionists and validated through controlled clinical research at Cornell, Purely Golden Foods delivers carefully prepared meals formulated with evidence, not trend.
Introduction.column2: We calibrate at the nutrient level, ensuring consistent amino acid ratios, fatty acid balance, and micronutrient density in every recipe. Nothing is incidental.

Standards.title: Our Standards
Standards.subtitle: These standards govern every step of our formulation, production, and verification.
Standards.box1Description: We verify nutrient content through testing each finished batch of food, ensuring every bowl meets the nutritional targets set out in our formulation model.
Standards.box2Description: Every production run is tested for nutrient density, confirming consistency from formulation to bowl.
Standards.box3Description: Our recipes are developed and reviewed by board-certified veterinary nutritionists, applying clinical reasoning to every decision.
Standards.box4Description: From sourcing to cooking, we apply strict quality controls that include pathogen screening, temperature management, and full traceability.

DiscoverPlans.title: Founding Cohort: Now Enrolling in Boston
DiscoverPlans.description: Clinical-grade nutrition, launching first in Boston. We’re enrolling a limited number of dogs into our founding cohort to ensure careful oversight and quality control.

Ingredients.title: Ingredient Library
Ingredients.description: Each recipe uses a specific selection of these ingredients. To learn more, view our detailed <recipeBreakdowns>recipe breakdowns</recipeBreakdowns>.

turkey.name: Turkey
turkey.description: A lean, highly digestible protein serving as a primary amino acid source. Favorable fat profile, suited for adult maintenance and controlled fat intake.

lamb.name: Lamb
lamb.description: A nutrient-dense red meat contributing essential amino acids, iron, and zinc. Supports protein diversity and palatability while maintaining controlled macronutrient ratios.

cod.name: Cod
cod.description: A low-fat, highly digestible fish protein. Selected for formulations requiring precise lipid control and fat tolerance.

beefLiver.name: Beef liver
beefLiver.description: A concentrated source of fat-soluble vitamins and trace minerals. Included in measured amounts to support micronutrient adequacy without exceeding safe upper limits.

chickenLiver.name: Chicken liver
chickenLiver.description: A functional organ providing bioavailable vitamins and minerals. Used selectively for micronutrient balance and formulation completeness.

egg.name: Egg
egg.description: A highly bioavailable protein containing all essential amino acids. Supports protein quality and digestibility within mixed-ingredient formulations.

pumpkin.name: Pumpkin
pumpkin.description: A soluble fiber source supporting stool consistency and digestive tolerance. Contributes fermentable fiber without altering macronutrient balance.

sweetPotato.name: Sweet potato
sweetPotato.description: A complex carbohydrate providing dietary fiber and sustained energy. Supports caloric structure and texture while maintaining digestibility.

brownRice.name: Brown rice
brownRice.description: A digestible carbohydrate used in select formulations. Contributes structured energy and supports dietary tolerance in balanced proportions.

butternutSquash.name: Butternut squash
butternutSquash.description: A carbohydrate and fiber source contributing natural beta-carotene. Included for functional diversity rather than caloric density.

spinach.name: Spinach
spinach.description: A low-inclusion leafy green providing trace micronutrients. Used for nutrient diversity, not as a primary fiber or calorie source.

blueberries.name: Blueberries
blueberries.description: A natural source of polyphenols, included in small quantities. Added for compositional diversity, not therapeutic effect.

cucumber.name: Cucumber
cucumber.description: A low-calorie, high-moisture vegetable supporting hydration and texture without materially altering macronutrient balance.

parsley.name: Parsley
parsley.description: Included in small amounts for micronutrient diversity and aromatic balance.

dill.name: Dill
dill.description: A culinary herb at low inclusion, contributing aroma and palatability without impacting nutritional targets.

omega3FishOil.name: Omega-3 fish oil
omega3FishOil.description: A purified source of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids. Added in measured amounts to support fatty acid balance and formulation consistency.

canolaOil.name: Canola oil
canolaOil.description: A neutral fat source used to calibrate total dietary fat levels. Supports precise lipid balance where lower overall fat is required.

flaxseedChiaSeed.name: Flaxseed and chia seed
flaxseedChiaSeed.description: Plant-based sources of fiber and alpha-linolenic acid (ALA). Used in small amounts to contribute to fiber diversity and fatty acid balance.

Precision.title:
Precision
in Every Batch
Precision.description: Fresh ingredients are variable by nature. Protein content differs. Produce changes with the season. Even when a formulation is fixed, raw inputs are not. For a company that refuses to compromise on precision, the work doesn't stop at formulation. Every batch is tested to verify that meals meet Association of American Feed Control Officials (AAFCO) vitamin and mineral requirements—not just on paper, but in the food itself. This is how we deliver consistency where it matters most: in what your dog actually consumes.
Precision.imageAlt: Freshly prepared nutritious meal with measuring spoon

Research.title: Clinical Validation
Research.description1: Every formulation decision is informed by controlled preclinical research conducted with Cornell University. Our research set out to observe how nutrition interacts with digestion, metabolism, and inflammatory balance.
Research.valueProp1Description: Assessed through daily fecal scoring (Purina 1–7 scale), monitoring consistency, variability, and digestive tolerance over time.
Research.valueProp2Description: Screening for diet-responsive physiological signals performed via diagnostic bloodwork, including serum chemistry, C-reactive protein (CRP), fructosamine, and IL-6.
Research.valueProp3Description: Monitored using standardized veterinary scoring systems to assess notable changes throughout the feeding period.
Research.step1Description1: Nutritional balance starts with formulation, but that alone doesn't guarantee what reaches the bowl.
Research.step1Description2: We test every batch against Association of American Feed Control Officials (AAFCO) vitamin and mineral standards before it ships.
Research.step2Description1: Same formulation. Same nutritional profile. Every batch, every bowl.
Research.step2Description2: We use controlled nutrient calibration, portion guidance, and batch-level verification to account for natural variation in sourcing, seasonality, and production.
Research.step3Description1: Fiber content, ingredient selection, and preparation methods are all engineered to support digestive stability.
Research.step3Description2: Each formulation decision is guided by what we've learned through our research with Cornell, validating digestive outcomes with standardized scoring and clinical observation.
Research.disclaimer: Observations from this evaluation inform formulation development and do not constitute therapeutic or disease-related claims.

UnderstandingFormulation.title: Understanding the Formulation
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq1Question: What does "clinical validation" mean for PGF?
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq1Answer: Controlled preclinical research conducted at Cornell University under standardized conditions. Our eight-week feeding evaluation assessed palatability, digestive tolerance, and physiological markers in adult dogs—generating objective data to inform formulation decisions. This is observational research, not an establishment therapeutic efficacy.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq2Question: Which biomarkers are measured, and why?
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq2Answer: CRP and IL-6 as indicators of inflammatory balance. Daily fecal scoring for digestive tolerance. Microbiome sequencing for microbial diversity. These markers reflect measurable physiological responses to diet.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq3Answer: Using the Purina Fecal Scoring System, a standardized 1–7 scale used in veterinary research. A score of 2 indicates ideal consistency. Daily scoring allows us to track digestive tolerance and variability over time.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq4Answer: A diverse gut microbiome is associated with more efficient digestion, nutrient synthesis, and balanced immune signaling. Our formulations include fermentable fibers and polyphenols to promote microbial diversity and short-chain fatty acid production. We do not claim to treat or prevent disease.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq5Question: Why measure inflammation in healthy dogs?
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq5Answer: Low-grade systemic inflammation is common and often diet-influenced. Monitoring CRP and IL-6 allows us to observe whether formulation correlates with shifts in inflammatory balance.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq6Question: How does PGF differ from prescription diets?
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq6Answer: Prescription diets manage diagnosed conditions and require veterinary authorization. PGF is formulated for adult maintenance in healthy dogs; we optimize for digestibility, gut stability, and nutrient adequacy, not to treat a specific disease.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq7Answer: Fresh ingredients vary. Every production batch is tested after preparation against AAFCO vitamin and mineral standards, confirming that finished meals meet nutrient targets regardless of seasonal or sourcing variation.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq8Answer: Board-certified veterinary nutritionists (ECVCN-credentialed). Research protocols are conducted under IACUC approval at Cornell, ensuring compliance with animal welfare and research governance standards.
UnderstandingFormulation.formulationFaq9Answer: Yes. We intend to share findings through appropriate channels as analysis is completed. Academic collaborators do not commercially endorse products, and we are committed to transparent reporting regardless of outcome.

Hero.title: About Us
Hero.subtitle: The science, standards, and clinical logic behind how our food is formulated.

Values.card1Description: True care begins with precision. Every formulation is developed using clinical nutrition principles, measured inputs, and documented processes. Pharmaceutical-grade discipline, applied to fresh food.
Values.card2Description: Nutrition should be grounded in evidence, not assumption. Our formulation decisions are guided by controlled clinical research at Cornell University, where digestive tolerance, palatability, and physiological markers are assessed under standardized conditions.
Values.card3Description: Small breeds don't simply eat less. They metabolize differently. Faster digestive transit. Higher stool variability. Narrower margins for nutrient error. Our formulations are engineered for their specific physiology, not scaled down from larger breeds.

Leadership.paragraph1: Purely Golden Foods didn't start as a business idea. It started as a practice of paying closer attention. Over time, I became less interested in quick answers and more interested in how bodies actually respond. Slowly. Unevenly. With far more nuance than most systems account for. That shift changed how I think about care, nourishment, and responsibility.
Leadership.paragraph2: PGF reflects that mindset. We take our time. We measure what matters. We don't rush outcomes or simplify complexity to make something easier to sell. This is food made with respect—for dogs, for their physiology, and for the people who care deeply about doing things right.

Mission.paragraph1: Ingredients are selected for function, not trend.
Mission.paragraph2: Digestible proteins for amino acid adequacy. Soluble fibers for gut motility. Targeted omega-3s for fatty acid balance. Each component earns its place through formulation architecture and evidence. Nothing is included for marketing appeal.
Mission.subsectionText: Nutrition is only meaningful if the body responds. Stool consistency, sustained energy levels, inflammatory markers moving in the right direction. We formulate for outcomes that can be seen, measured, and verified—not benefits that exist only as advertising copy.
Mission.title: The Inputs We Control

Commitment.card1: <bold>Evidence over trend.</bold> Every decision is guided by nutritional science and documented standards.
Commitment.card2: <bold>Measured care.</bold> Meals designed to support physiological outcomes, not marketing claims.
Commitment.card3: <bold>Uncompromising integrity.</bold> We hold ourselves to the same standard expected in human medicine.
Commitment.title: Our Commitment

Experts.academicBody: Academic collaborators do not commercially endorse products.
